title: Mixed-dimensional quantum Monte Carlo studies of M-point moiré materials
authors: Dumitru Călugăru, Konstantinos Vasiliou, Haoyu Hu, B. Andrei Bernevig, Werner Krauth, S. A. Parameswaran
abstract: A new moiré-material platform has recently been proposed based on twisting two-dimensional triangular-lattice monolayers whose low-energy states lie at the three M points of the Brillouin zone. Continuum models derived from extensive ab initio simulations suggest that electrons in the conduction bands of one such M-point moiré material, twisted AA-stacked SnSe$_2$, realize a three-orbital Hubbard model with orbitally-selective, quasi-one-dimensional (quasi-1D) hopping, protected by a projective mirror symmetry. Here, we show that the resulting "mixed-dimensional" limit -- in which the hopping is exactly quasi-1D in each valley, while the valleys are coupled by interactions into a fully two-dimensional network -- can be sampled with Stochastic Series Expansion (SSE) quantum Monte Carlo (QMC) without a sign problem at any filling. We develop an efficient new SSE QMC algorithm that combines custom global updates with parallel tempering to overcome the equilibration challenges posed by the mixed-dimensional setting. We then use this algorithm to explore the phase diagram of M-point twisted AA-stacked SnSe$_2$. Over extended and realistic ranges of twist angles and interaction strengths, we find that at integer fillings the system supports correlated insulators whose nature and strength depend strongly on angle. At certain commensurate fractional fillings, we further find evidence for Wigner-Mott insulators. We analytically account for the main features observed numerically using a strong-coupling description. Finally, we discuss perturbations away from the mixed-dimensional limit and the possibility of applying our method to other realizations of mixed-dimensional Hubbard models.
summary: The research investigates strongly correlated physics in M-point moiré materials using an advanced Stochastic Series Expansion Quantum Monte Carlo technique. By developing a sign-problem-free algorithm capable of handling mixed dimensionality, the authors map out phase diagrams revealing correlated insulating and Wigner-Mott phases. This work advances computational methods for studying complex electronic correlations in novel 2D material platforms.
main_problem: To study the strongly interacting physics of M-point moiré materials, particularly exploring regimes beyond idealized limits like intermediate coupling or away from integer fillings.
main_result: The authors successfully applied a sign-problem-free Quantum Monte Carlo method to map out the phase diagram, finding evidence for correlated insulators and Wigner-Mott states in twisted SnSe2.
method: Stochastic Series Expansion (SSE) Quantum Monte Carlo (QMC), which was adapted with custom global updates and parallel tempering to handle the mixed-dimensional nature of the system.
model_or_system: The physical platform is AA-stacked $	ext{SnSe}_2$, modeled by a three-orbital Hubbard Hamiltonian exhibiting quasi-one-dimensional hopping within valleys, coupled into a fully two-dimensional network. The model captures strong electronic correlations in this moiré superlattice.
key_observables:
Inverse compressibility ($\partial\mu/\partial
u$), charge and spin stiffnesses ($ho_C, ho_S$), susceptibility components, and the spin-spin correlation function $S(i)$.
important_parameters:
Twist angle ($	heta$), interaction strength ($U, V$), filling factor ($
u$), and temperature/inverse temperature ($ eta$).
main_assumptions: The analysis often neglects Hund's and anti-Hund's couplings due to their small magnitude or antagonistic nature; the method requires specialized inter-chain updates for full equilibration.
figures_summary: Figures display quantitative results across various parameters, showing how observables like compressibility and stiffnesses change with filling and temperature in the mixed-dimensional regime. Benchmarking figures compare SSE against ED/DQMC to validate the algorithm's accuracy.
paper_structure: The paper systematically develops the model Hamiltonian, details the necessary adaptations of the SSE QMC method (including inter-chain updates), benchmarks this method against exact and determinantal methods, and finally applies it to map out the phase diagram by calculating various thermodynamic observables.
why_interesting: This work provides a powerful numerical tool (sign-problem-free QMC) to study complex, quasi-low-dimensional correlated electron systems that are highly relevant for understanding emergent phenomena in 2D materials.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Parameter regions without a sign problem for the AA t-SnSe2 Hamiltonian. We highlight the regions in the space of next-nearest-neighbor inter-chain hopping t⊥and electron filling per moiré unit cell ν in which the AA t-SnSe2 Hamil- tonian can be sampled within QMC without a sign problem. Here, ν denotes the filling of the six conduction-band flavors of AA t-SnSe2, arising from three valleys and two spin species, so that 0 ≤ν ≤6. The limits ν = 0 and ν = 6 correspond to empty and fully filled conduction bands, respectively, i.e. band insulators (BIs).

FIG. 2. Tight-binding model of the first conduction band of AA t-SnSe2. The lattice and the Wannier orbitals are shown in (a). The black parallelogram marks the conventional unit cell and the black arrows the moiré lattice vectors. Orbitals are shown as disks, with valley index η encoded by color: red, green, and blue correspond to η = 0, 1, 2, respectively. The leading quasi-1D hopping amplitude t is shown for val- ley η = 0, and the colored lines indicate the chains along which electrons tunnel within each valley. The interaction is fully 2D and of density-density form, with onsite three-orbital Hubbard interactions and a nearest-neighbor repulsion shown in gray. As a coarse...

FIG. 3. Parameters of the AA t-SnSe2 Hamiltonian in Eq. (1). The plot shows the average onsite ( ¯U) and nearest-neighbor ( ¯V ) Coulomb repulsion parameters for the different twist an- gles θ and screening lengths ξ studied in this work (see also Section I). The twist angle is encoded by color, while the marker type distinguishes the different screening lengths ξ. The oblique gray lines are contours of constant effective on- site Hubbard repulsion Ueff [111]. The dashed orange line bounds the yellow region, which is free of a sign problem at half filling within DQMC [76]. The idealized strong-coupling model with only onsite Hubbard repulsion is shown by the square black marker.

FIG. 5. Off-diagonal loop update on a single chain. The top-left panel shows a fragment of an initial SSE configuration containing the full world lines of four orbitals. The orbital states in |α⟩are indicated at the bottom; along the world lines they are represented schematically by dashed (0), single-arrow (↑/↓), and doubled (↑↓) lines. The subsequent panels, read clockwise, illustrate an off-diagonal update initiated by inserting a worm, highlighted in red, on one world line. The Fock state of the world line changes at the two worm ends (in the first panel, the state changes from 0 to ↓to 0 along the world line containing the worm). One end of the worm, the worm head, is then propagated...

FIG. 6. Inter-chain update. (a) shows two intersecting chains (red and blue) of the lattice from Fig. 2(a) between which a particle transfer is attempted at the site marked by the black circle. The third chain (green) passing through the same site is shown for completeness but is unaffected by the update. (b) presents a schematic weight ( ˜ W) landscape of SSE configurations. Initially, the red (blue) chain contains N1 (N2) particles. Transferring a particle from the red to the blue chain changes the occupations to (N1 −1, N2 + 1).
